title: Design collaboration tool review: Figma, Adobe CC Libraries and In Vision 2026 comparison
description: In-depth review of 2026 design collaboration tools Figma, Adobe CC Libraries, and In Vision, comparing real-time collaboration, version management, and design system features to provide efficient collaboration solutions for design teams. Learn more about features and pricing comparisons to find the plan that's right for you! Professional reviews to help you make decisions!
content:
The Necessity of Design Collaborationoration Tools and Team Efficiency

Modern design work has shifted from independent creation to team collaboration. Designers, copywriters, product managers, and developers need real-time communication, file sharing, version tracking, and feedback management. Design collaboration tools solve traditional collaboration pain points: chaotic file transfers, scattered feedback, difficult version management, and inconsistent design standards.

According to industry research, design teams using collaboration tools reduce project cycles by 30%, improve communication efficiency by 50%, and increase design standard consistency by 80%. The 2026 design collaboration market shows a three-party landscape with Figma leading real-time collaboration, Adobe CC Libraries providing ecosystem integration, and In Vision specializing in prototype feedback.

Figma 2026 In-depth Review

The Revolutionary Real-time Collaborationoration Design Tool

Figma is a disruptor in the design collaboration field, reshaping design workflows with cloud-based real-time collaboration. By 2026, Figma has become the mainstream tool for UI/UX design and is expanding into graphic design and publication design.

Core Collaborationoration Features

  • Real-time Multi-user Editing: Multiple users edit the same file simultaneously, with real-time display of editing positions
  • Comments and Annotations: Direct comments on designs, with @mentions for relevant people
  • Version History: Automatic version saving, with rollback to any version
  • Observation Mode: Follow others' perspectives, real-time viewing of their design process
  • Permission Management: Tiered permissions for editing, viewing, and commenting

Design System Features

  • Component Library: Reusable components, changes update globally
  • Style System: Unified management of colors, fonts, and effects styles
  • Variables: Design variables supporting multiple themes and states
  • Team Library: Team-shared components and style libraries

2026 New Features

  • AI-Assisted Design: AI automatic layout suggestions, component recommendations, and color scheme suggestions
  • Dev Mode: Developer view with automatic code specification generation
  • Fig Jam Extensions: Deep integration of design whiteboard and collaboration tools
  • Branching: Design file branch management, similar to Git branches

Print Design Adaptation

Figma's native positioning is UI/UX, but the 2026 version enhances print design features:

  • Print Colors: CMYK color support, print color palettes
  • Bleed and Crop: Print bleed marks, crop paths
  • High-resolution Output: PDF output supports 300dpi+

Some print designers have started using Figma for brochures and poster design.

Pricing

  • Free Tier: Personal use, 3 projects
  • Professional: $15/user/month, unlimited projects
  • Organization: $45/user/month, team libraries, advanced permissions
  • Enterprise: $75/user/month, SSO, advanced security

Adobe CC Libraries Review

The Collaborationoration Connector in Adobe Ecosystem

Adobe CC Libraries is Adobe Creative Cloud's cloud-based assets and styles collaboration platform, connecting Photoshop, Illustrator, In Design, and other Adobe applications.

Core Features

  • Cloud Asset Library: Cloud storage for colors, fonts, graphics, and styles
  • Team Sharing: Team library sharing, unified asset usage
  • Adobe App Integration: Direct access to library content in Photoshop, Illustrator, In Design
  • Version Management: Asset version tracking

Collaborationoration Scenarios

  • Color Unification: Team color palettes shared in cloud, brand color consistency
  • Font Licensing: Adobe Fonts cloud activation, unified team fonts
  • Graphic Assets: Team-shared graphics, icons, photos
  • Style Synchronization: Cross-app paragraph style and layer style sync

Comparison with Figma

  • Advantage: Deep Adobe ecosystem integration, seamless print professional applications
  • Disadvantage: No real-time multi-user editing, collaboration less fluid than Figma

Pricing

CC Libraries is a built-in feature of Adobe CC subscription, no extra cost:

  • Complete CC: $59.99/month (includes CC Libraries)

In Vision Review

Professional Platform for Prototypes and Feedback

In Vision is a pioneer in design collaboration, focusing on prototype creation and feedback management, serving UI/UX and graphic design teams.

Core Features

  • Prototype Creation: Convert designs to interactive prototypes with click navigation and animation transitions
  • Feedback System: Design annotation comments, centralized feedback management
  • Version Management: Design version comparison, history tracking
  • Board Whiteboard: Design whiteboard for asset collection and organization
  • Freehand: Real-time collaboration whiteboard for team brainstorming

Collaborationoration Workflow

Typical In Vision collaboration process:

  1. Designer uploads design files (supports Sketch, Photoshop, XD files)
  2. Add interactive hotspots, create prototype
  3. Share link with team and clients
  4. Team and clients annotate comments on prototype
  5. Designer organizes feedback, updates design

DSM Design System Management

In Vision DSM (Design System Manager) is a design system management tool:

  • Component Library: Design component documentation
  • Code Sync: Design component and code component synchronization
  • Brand Guidelines: Brand visual standards documentation

Pricing

  • Free Tier: Personal use, basic prototypes
  • Professional: $15/user/month, unlimited prototypes
  • Enterprise: Custom pricing, DSM, advanced security

Limitations

In Vision has no design editing functionality, relies on external design tools (Sketch, Photoshop, XD). As Figma integrated prototyping in 2026, In Vision market share declined.

Design Collaborationoration Tools Comparison Analysis Table

Design EditingCompleteNone (Adobe apps)None

Team Cost$15-75/person/month$60/person/month (CC)$15/person/month

2026 Design Collaborationoration Trends Forecast

1. Design Editing and Collaborationoration Integration

Figma model becomes mainstream: design tools with built-in collaboration features, no need for external collaboration platforms. Adobe will enhance CC collaboration capabilities, In Vision faces consolidation pressure.

2. AI Collaborationoration Assistance

AI application in collaboration: automatic feedback organization, AI design suggestions, conflict detection. Figma AI has launched collaboration assistance features.

3. Design System Cloud Standardization

Design systems (components, styles, standards) cloud sharing: team libraries, enterprise-level design system management. Figma Team Libraries and In Vision DSM lead.

4. Print Design Collaborationoration Extension

Figma and other tools enhance print design collaboration: CMYK color sharing, print standards cloud management, prepress collaboration.

5. Development Collaborationoration Deepening

Design and development collaboration: automatic code generation from design specs, component design sync with code components. Figma Dev Mode leads.

Selection Recommendations

  • UI/UX Design Teams: Figma design + collaboration + prototype integration, optimal efficiency
  • Adobe Ecosystem Design Teams: CC Libraries seamless integration, suitable for print design
  • Prototype Feedback Management: In Vision professional feedback system, suitable for client communication
  • Cross-functional Teams: Figma real-time collaboration supports designers, product managers, and developers participating together
  • Design System Management: In Vision DSM enterprise-level design system documentation

Recommend selecting based on team design tool preferences, collaboration depth needs, and budget. Figma has become mainstream, Adobe teams can combine CC Libraries + Figma.
author: Design Team Management Expert
